function.name: create_payment_intent
function.description: Creates a PaymentIntent object for processing payments. A PaymentIntent guides you through the process of collecting a payment from your customer. It tracks charge attempts and payment state changes throughout the payment lifecycle.

amount.title: Amount
amount.description: Amount intended to be collected by this PaymentIntent. A positive integer representing how much to charge in the smallest currency unit (e.g., 100 cents for $1.00).

currency.title: Currency Code
currency.description: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase. Must be a supported currency.

customer.title: Customer ID
customer.description: ID of the Customer this PaymentIntent belongs to, if one exists. Must be a valid Stripe customer ID starting with 'cus_'.

payment_method.title: Payment Method ID
payment_method.description: ID of the payment method to attach to this PaymentIntent. Must be a valid Stripe payment method ID starting with 'pm_'.

payment_method_types.title: Payment Method Types
payment_method_types.description: The list of payment method types that this PaymentIntent is allowed to use. If not provided, defaults to ['card'].

confirm.title: Confirm Payment
confirm.description: Set to true to attempt to confirm this PaymentIntent immediately. If the PaymentIntent has a payment_method attached and requires confirmation, setting confirm=true will attempt to confirm and capture.

automatic_payment_methods.title: Automatic Payment Methods
automatic_payment_methods.description: When enabled, this PaymentIntent will accept payment methods enabled in the Dashboard and compatible with this PaymentIntent's parameters.

enabled.description: Whether automatic payment methods are enabled for this PaymentIntent.

allow_redirects.description: Controls whether redirect-based payment methods should be enabled.

capture_method.title: Capture Method
capture_method.description: Controls when the funds will be captured. 'automatic' captures immediately, 'manual' requires a separate capture request.

confirmation_method.title: Confirmation Method
confirmation_method.description: How the PaymentIntent will be confirmed. 'automatic' is used when confirming from client-side, 'manual' from server-side.

description.title: Description
description.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. Useful for storing additional information.

receipt_email.title: Receipt Email
receipt_email.description: Email address to send the receipt to. If set, a receipt will be sent when the payment succeeds.

setup_future_usage.title: Setup Future Usage
setup_future_usage.description: Indicates that you intend to make future payments with this PaymentIntent's payment method. This will attach the payment method to the customer.

statement_descriptor.title: Statement Descriptor
statement_descriptor.description: For non-card charges, the statement description. For card charges, use statement_descriptor_suffix.

statement_descriptor_suffix.title: Statement Descriptor Suffix
statement_descriptor_suffix.description: Provides information about a card payment that customers see on their statements.

application_fee_amount.title: Application Fee
application_fee_amount.description: The amount of the application fee in cents to be applied to the payment. Only for Connect.

transfer_data.title: Transfer Data
transfer_data.description: The parameters used to automatically create a Transfer when the payment succeeds. Connect only.

destination.description: ID of the connected account to transfer funds to.

amount.description: Amount to transfer. If not set, the entire amount is transferred.

on_behalf_of.title: On Behalf Of
on_behalf_of.description: The Stripe account ID for which these funds are intended. Connect only.

return_url.title: Return URL
return_url.description: The URL to redirect your customer back to after they authenticate or cancel on the payment method's app or site.
